summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the gossamer-serve backend inside the app so users have a single install experience.
summary.outcome: Created GitHub Actions release workflow, Homebrew Cask template, and electron-builder configuration. Updated the Electron main process to spawn the bundled server via utilityProcess, with extraResources config to include the server's dist/node_modules/prisma at build time. Changes were committed and pushed to origin/README.

repo: The project has two separate packages: a root-level Node.js server (gossamer-serve) and an Electron frontend in app/. They were originally decoupled with no bundling relationship.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary paths, so the bundled directory layout must preserve this relative structure.
prisma was in devDependencies but is required at runtime for pushSchema calls — needed to be moved to dependencies.
The Electron app connects to gossamer-serve as an external process; the server URL is configured in the renderer.

code.finding: Newly created CJS wrapper needed because utilityProcess.fork() requires a CommonJS entry point but the server is ESM — wrapper dynamically import()s the ESM server.
Added extraResources config to copy root dist/, node_modules/, and prisma/ into Contents/Resources/server/ inside the app bundle.
Updated to spawn gossamer-serve as a utilityProcess in production, pointing at the bundled server-launcher.cjs in process.resourcesPath.
Workflow installs root deps, rebuilds native modules against Electron's ABI, prunes devDeps, then runs electron-builder to produce arm64 and x64 DMGs.

workflow: Homebrew Cask distribution requires a separate tap repo (homebrew-gossamer) and SHA256 hashes of each DMG artifact, which must be computed after the release runs.
electron-builder extraResources is the mechanism to include non-JS assets (server build output, node_modules) inside the .app bundle.
Native module rebuilding against Electron's ABI must happen in CI before packaging, not just a standard npm install.

friction: Initial workflow only targeted the app/ directory, missing the server entirely — caught only after the first commit was pushed.
Three non-trivial bundling challenges surfaced during implementation: import.meta.url path resolution, CJS/ESM boundary for utilityProcess, and prisma being in devDependencies.
open_items: The app is unsigned — users must bypass Gatekeeper on first launch via System Settings. Proper code signing requires an Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and adding signing secrets to the workflow.
SHA256 placeholders in homebrew/gossamer.rb must be filled in manually after the first release runs.
The Homebrew tap repo (nosman/homebrew-gossamer) still needs to be created and the Cask file copied into it.
The second commit (bundled server changes) was staged but the transcript ends before confirming a successful push — may need verification.
No testing of the bundled app was done — the utilityProcess server launch path is untested.
